analysis.reasoning: Core message: Lavrov jokes that the US cannot have a color revolution because it lacks an American embassy—implying the US engineers color revolutions abroad—then adds a seemingly benevolent remark hoping the US does not fall into crisis. Evaluation: RU1 (West immoral/hostile/decadent): The joke insinuates US interference in other states’ internal affairs via color revolutions, portraying the US as a hostile, subversive actor; this aligns with RU1. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system): Not present; no mention of multipolarity or alternative institutions. RU3 (Russia is a benevolent partner): The closing line expresses concern for the US avoiding crisis, but it is more rhetorical politeness than positioning Russia as a partner promoting peace/solidarity; insufficient to claim RU3. RU4 (Russian history admirable): Not referenced. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront): Not referenced. RU6 (Russia culture/nature): Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes RU1.
